div.p: Malcolm king of Scots to Uhtred son of Fergus, and Gilbert his brother, and Ralph son of Dunegal, and Domnall his brother, and to all his other worthy men of the whole of Galloway and Clydesdale, Greeting: Know that I have justly given my firm peace to all those men who go or shall go to Galloway to lodge or dwell in the Land of Dunrod, which Fergus gave to the church of Holyrood in Edinburgh in perpetual alms and affirmed by his charter, which Uhtred his son also confirmed by his charter, whose charters I myself have also confirmed. I therefore firmly forbid that any man presume unjustly to vex or disturb anyone travelling to the said land by reason of dwelling in it, or dwelling in the same land by consent and will of the canons of the aforesaid church, or departing from that land in the fealty of the same [canons]. And no-one may remain in the same land by anyone’s advowson or lordship against the will of the said canons, on pain of my forfeiture of ten pounds. As witnesses, Alured abbot of Stirling, Robert prior of May, Ingram the chancellor, Walter the steward, son of Alan, John de Vaux. At Clackmannan.
